config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, hak individu harus menjadi batas yang tidak boleh dikorbankan begitu saja atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena tanpa perlindungan hak, mayoritas bisa menindas minoritas. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi seharusnya dicapai dengan cara yang tetap menghormati martabat, kebebasan, dan perlakuan adil bagi setiap orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang kuat justru dibangun dari individu-individu yang haknya dilindungi, bukan dari pengorbanan hak yang berlebihan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should automatically outrank the rights of the individual, because in the United States we have seen how easily "the common good" can be used to justify abuse of minorities. Public safety, health, and fairness matter, but they have to be pursued within clear limits that protect free speech, due process, privacy, and equal treatment. If individual rights are treated as disposable, society does not become stronger; it becomes more willing to sacrifice people who are politically inconvenient. The better standard is that society can ask for real obligations from individuals, but only when the burden is necessary, proportional, and does not erase basic rights.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum, karena di Indonesia juga kita sering melihat alasan “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai untuk membenarkan perlakuan yang tidak adil. Kepentingan sosial memang penting untuk kesehatan, ketertiban, dan solidaritas, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar seperti kebebasan berpendapat, perlindungan hukum, dan martabat manusia. Kalau hak individu dianggap bisa dikorbankan kapan saja, yang lebih kuat justru mudah menekan yang lemah atas nama mayoritas. Menurut saya, ukuran yang benar adalah kepentingan bersama boleh dibatasi sementara, tetapi hanya bila benar-benar perlu, proporsional, dan tidak merusak hak dasar orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the individual as a general rule, because in the United States that kind of language has too often been used to excuse censorship, discrimination, and government overreach. At the same time, I do not think individual rights are absolute in every case; public health, safety, and basic order can justify limits when they are narrow and clearly necessary. The key difference is that society should have to prove its case, not just invoke “the common good” and expect people to give up their rights. If we get that balance wrong, the result is not a stronger society but a more authoritarian one.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu karena di Indonesia istilah “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menutup-nutupi ketidakadilan terhadap orang kecil atau kelompok minoritas. Memang benar hak individu tidak absolut, misalnya saat ada ancaman kesehatan publik atau keselamatan, tetapi pembatasannya harus sangat jelas, perlu, dan proporsional, bukan sekadar karena mayoritas ingin menang sendiri. Buat saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi hak dasar sambil tetap menjaga ketertiban bersama. Jika hak dasar bisa dikalahkan begitu saja, yang terjadi bukan solidaritas sosial, melainkan dominasi yang dibungkus bahasa kepentingan umum.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I still reject the idea that society should generally outrank the individual, because in the United States that logic has often been used to excuse segregation, censorship, and heavy-handed government power. At the same time, I do not believe individual rights are unlimited; things like public health measures, emergency rules, and basic safety laws can be justified when they are narrow, necessary, and accountable. What matters is that society has to make a serious case before restricting rights, instead of treating “the common good” as a blank check. A healthy society protects people first and asks for sacrifice only when it is truly proportional and unavoidable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
